steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Solve $x^2 - 2x - 18 = 0$ using the quadratic formula with $a = 1$, $b = -2$, $c = -18$.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: $x = \frac{2 \pm \sqrt{4 + 72}}{2} = \frac{2 \pm \sqrt{76}}{2}$. Since $\sqrt{76} = 2\sqrt{19}$, this simplifies to $x = 1 \pm \sqrt{19}$. The positive solution is $1 + \sqrt{19}$, so $k = 19$.
